name: Focaccia-Fresh Rosemary Onion
recipeIngredient: 2 large Spanish onions, cooked in 2 tbsp olive oil
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine all dough ingredients except the cooked onion in your bread machine.
2. Program for knead and first rise. Press start.
3. Strain the onions and return the liquid to the bowl.
4. At the end of the final knead, add the onion (without liquid) to the machine.
5. Restart and knead only until the onion is roughly mixed in. The dough will be wet, and the onion will remain in clumps, sticking out of the dough.
6. Turn the dough out onto a well-floured board and knead briefly by hand to form a ball.
7. Oil a pizza pan or baking sheet.
8. Place the dough on the prepared pan and pat into a 12-inch disk.
9. Coat the top of the dough with onion juices. Cover with plastic wrap and let it rise at room temperature until doubled in bulk, or refrigerate overnight for a more flavorful bread with a heartier interior.
10. Preheat the oven to 450°F with the rack in the center position.
11. Just before baking, use your fingertips to gently make indentations in the surface.
12. Drizzle on the oil and sprinkle with salt and pepper.
13. Bake for 10 minutes, then reduce the temperature to 350°F and bake for another 12 to 15 minutes, or until the focaccia is golden brown. Serve hot or at room temperature.
